lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: What can I help you with, dear?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you and what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: I'm Abigaila, from Morytania. It's a terrible place, run by the vampyres. They visit the towns and collect everyone's blood for food!

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: I tried for years to escape, with my son, Suro, to make a better life for ourselves in Misthalin. I scraped together enough money to bribe a boatman to get us out.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: The journey was tough. Some men in pink hoods attacked our boat, shouting at us to go back where we belonged, and to keep out of their country. In the struggle, the boat overturned.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: It was dark, and I couldn't find Suro in the water. I swam as deep as I could, until my lungs screamed, but I couldn't find him. He wasn't good at swimming.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: Eventually I got myself to the shore. The sun was rising, and I found Suro there. On the shore, where the water had dumped his... body.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: I've done alright in Misthalin, I guess. I know a bit of magic, and the local gentry pay me well for my scrying skills. I'm good at turning a profit on Grand Exchange trades too.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: Anyway, I'm able to rent a room in Lumbridge, and to buy food. I'm not being a burden on anyone, and there are no vampyres coming for my blood.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: But I haven't forgotten my people in the east. There's one vampyre there who runs a place she calls the Theatre of Blood, challenging humans to face monsters in her arena.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: My husband went there, trying to win freedom from the blood-gatherers by surviving her arena, but he never returned. No-one does.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: So now I pass my spare time scrying on the Theatre of Blood, watching other adventurers there. You can have a look, if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to look in your scrying ball.

lines.speaker: Abigaila
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't let you use my scrying ball now, dear. Clear out everything you're carrying, and make sure you're not sick or poisoned.
